description:
'''Rage''' (also known as '''rage music''', '''rage beats''' or '''rage rap''') is a [[subgenre]] of [[trap music]] that originated in the [[United States]] in the late 2010s. The early rage sound was characterized by short, looping, [[Stereophonic sound#Pseudo-stereo|stereo-widened]], [[future bass]]-influenced [[synthesizer]] lead hooks and basic, energetic trap [[rhythm]]s. Among the pioneers of the style were [[Playboi Carti]], [[Lil Uzi Vert]] and [[Trippie Redd]].  

By the 2020s, the genre had developed through the influence of Carti's 2020 album ''[[Whole Lotta Red]]'' and his [[Opium (record label)|Opium]] label with artists such as [[Destroy Lonely]] and [[Ken Carson]], along with [[Yeat]] and [[SoFaygo]], who contributed to the popularization of the genre. Rage rap would evolve into a more extreme and experimental sound with artists such as [[OsamaSon]], [[Che (rapper)|Che]], [[2Slimey]], and [[Edward Skeletrix]]. This style was defined by the use of bass heavy [[Tr-808|808s]], screamed [[Auto-Tune|autotune]] vocals and aggressive production.   

According to ''[[Pitchfork (website)|Pitchfork]]'', American rapper [[Slayr]] developed an accessible form of the genre on his 2025 mixtape [[Half Blood (mixtape)|''Half Blood'']]. Several underground rappers later released rage albums such as [[Nettspend]]'s 2026 debut studio album ''[[Early Life Crisis]]''.
